refresh_notes: WebSearch/WebFetch unavailable this refresh. Calendar dates verified against standard BEA/BLS/Eurostat/ISM release schedules. Price data verified from SPY.json (648.57 as of 2026-03-22T20:05Z) and GLD.json (413.38 as of 2026-03-22T20:05Z). Regime context from user briefing: VIX 26.78, CNN F&G 15, WTI $98.23, Gold $4,491, EUR/USD 1.159, Fed 3.5-3.75% (1 cut signaled). EU DST starts Mar 29 — times verified for both pre/post DST windows.

opportunities.event_name: US Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index — February 2026
opportunities.rationale: PCE is the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, released 10 days after FOMC held at 3.5-3.75% and raised PCE forecast to 2.7%. With WTI at $98.23 driving energy cost pass-through, a hot print (core PCE >0.4% MoM) would kill remaining rate-cut hopes and send US500 toward 6,300; a soft print (<0.2%) would trigger relief rally toward 6,650 as 1-cut pricing firms. SPY at 648.57 (S&P ~6,486) sits well below 50-day MA (~6,818) — bearish structure intact. VIX 26.78 (elevated) ensures outsized reaction. Invalidation: VIX drops below 20 before release, suggesting regime shift. NOTE: EU DST starts Mar 29 (Sunday after this release) — time is pre-DST CET (UTC+1), so 14:30 CET = 13:30 UTC = 08:30 ET.

opportunities.event_name: US Non-Farm Payrolls & Unemployment Rate — March 2026
opportunities.rationale: February NFP printed a soft 92K, sharply below expectations. March data will confirm whether the labour market is genuinely weakening under oil-driven stagflation or if Feb was a weather/seasonal anomaly. A sub-100K print would accelerate recession pricing and send US500 toward 6,200-6,300; a strong rebound (>200K) eases growth fears but keeps Fed on hold. SPY at 648.57 (S&P ~6,486), below 50-day MA ~6,818 — bear trend intact. VIX 26.78 ensures amplified reaction. Invalidation: Iran ceasefire materially shifts risk appetite before release. NOTE: Post EU-DST, 14:30 CEST = 12:30 UTC = 08:30 ET.

opportunities.event_name: OPEC+ Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee Meeting
opportunities.trade_setup: volatility spike — direction dependent on output decision
opportunities.rationale: With Strait of Hormuz disrupted (70% traffic drop) and WTI at $98.23, OPEC+ faces a binary decision: increase output to offset Iran supply loss (bearish oil, bullish equities) or maintain cuts to weaponise scarcity (bullish oil toward $110+, bearish equities). Goldman Sachs Brent target $110. Saudi Arabia's response is the key variable. WTI at $98.23 near 52-week high ~$100 — a supply boost would break the rally, output hold sends it to $105-110. Invalidation: Hormuz reopens before meeting. NOTE: Time approximate — OPEC+ meetings have no fixed start time; 13:00 CEST is when communiques typically emerge.

Eurozone inflation under dual pressure: oil-driven energy costs (WTI $98.23) pushing headline CPI higher vs weak domestic demand pulling core lower. A hot print (>2.8% YoY) constrains ECB easing and supports EUR/USD above 1.16; a soft print (<2.3%) opens door for April cut and pressures EUR toward 1.14. EUR/USD at 1.159, hovering near 50-day support. Invalidation: US PCE on Mar 28 causes such a large USD move that EU CPI reaction is muted. NOTE: Released on Tuesday Mar 31 post-DST, 11:00 CEST = 09:00 UTC.

ISM Manufacturing prints the same day as OPEC+ meeting, creating a double-catalyst session on April 1. A sub-50 contraction reading confirms the stagflation narrative (rising input prices from oil + shrinking activity) and accelerates equity selling. Expansion above 50 with tame prices-paid sub-component would be the most bullish combo. SPY at 648.57 (S&P ~6,486) below 50-day MA (~6,818). VIX 26.78 = elevated — outsized moves expected. Invalidation: OPEC+ headline dominates and ISM reaction is negligible. NOTE: 16:00 CEST = 14:00 UTC = 10:00 ET (post EU-DST).

opportunities.trade_setup: pullback buy on supply shock continuation
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on dip into 94-97 zone on any temporary de-escalation noise or pre-OPEC+ profit-taking. Exit immediately on confirmed ceasefire + Hormuz reopening or OPEC+ emergency output increase >1.5Mbpd. Do NOT add to position within 24h before OPEC+ meeting (Apr 1).
opportunities.rationale: Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic reduction is choking ~15Mbpd of global oil transit. Goldman Sachs target $110 Brent, JPMorgan warns sustained >$90 triggers 10-15% S&P correction. WTI at $98.23 near 52-week highs; a pullback into 94-97 (prior breakout zone above the $90 resistance shelf) offers R:R of 1:2.2 from midpoint $95.50 to $108 target with stop at $89.50. Stop below structural $90 level that held as resistance through Q4 2025. VIX 26.78 (elevated) — widen mental stop tolerance for intraday noise, keep position size at 2% risk. Invalidation: confirmed ceasefire, coordinated IEA SPR release, or daily close below $89.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long only if gold holds above $4,350 for 2 consecutive daily closes AND RSI(14) daily turns up from sub-30 territory. Do NOT enter pre-PCE (Mar 28) — wait for post-data confirmation. Scale: 50% initial, add remaining 50% on reclaim of $4,600. GLD ETF at 413.38 confirms spot near $4,491.
opportunities.rationale: Gold crashed 17% from $5,400 to $4,491 on USD strength + rate-cut repricing after FOMC signaled only 1 cut in 2026. However, the Iran war safe-haven bid and stagflation backdrop remain intact. The $4,350 area aligns with the November 2025 breakout level and estimated 200-day MA zone. R:R is 1:2.0 from midpoint ($4,425) to $4,850 (prior support-turned-resistance from early March). Stop below $4,180 respects the measured move target of the crash. GLD 7-day price action: 460.84 -> 413.38 (-10.3%), confirming aggressive selling pressure. VIX 26.78 = elevated — gold may see continued liquidation if margin calls persist. Invalidation: daily close below $4,180 or Fed signals 0 cuts for 2026.

opportunities.trade_setup: safe-haven USD continuation into event cluster
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter short at current levels (1.158-1.165) with reduced size ahead of PCE (Mar 28) and EU CPI (Mar 31). Add on hot US PCE or soft EU CPI. Exit on dovish Fed repricing or confirmed Iran de-escalation.
opportunities.rationale: EUR/USD at 1.159 caught between safe-haven USD demand (Iran war, VIX 26.78) and Eurozone energy vulnerability (Germany oil-import dependent, WTI $98.23). Fed held at 3.5-3.75% with hawkish 1-cut dot plot; ECB under pressure to cut on weak growth. Rate differential favours USD. The 1.1650 level is prior structure resistance and 20-day EMA area. Target 1.1300 aligns with January 2026 low. R:R = 1:1.8 from midpoint 1.1615. DOWNGRADED from MODERATE to LOW: two binary data releases (PCE Mar 28, EU CPI Mar 31) fall within the holding period, creating event risk that could reverse the trade on either print. CNN F&G at 15 (Extreme Fear) is contrarian — sudden risk-on reversal would hurt USD longs. Invalidation: daily close above 1.1750.
opportunities.regime_override_justification: Short USD pairs aligns with high-vol risk-off regime, but LOW conviction due to dual binary event risk within holding window and extreme fear contrarian signal.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter short on any bounce into 6,550-6,650 zone (prior support, now resistance). Requires daily close below 50-day MA (~6,818). Skip entry if VIX drops below 22 or Iran ceasefire is announced. SPY at 648.57 confirms index near 6,486.
opportunities.rationale: SPY closed at 648.57 on Mar 20, down from 670.79 on Mar 17 — a -3.3% decline in 3 sessions confirming accelerating selling pressure. S&P ~6,486 trades well below 50-day MA (~6,818) in confirmed downtrend. VIX 26.78 (elevated), CNN F&G 15 (Extreme Fear). The 6,550-6,650 zone was prior support that broke down and should act as resistance on any relief bounce. JPMorgan warns oil above $90 could trigger 10-15% correction from highs (~7,200), implying 6,100-6,200 target. R:R = 1:2.5 from 6,600 midpoint. Conviction LOW because Extreme Fear at 15 is a strong contrarian signal — historically, readings below 20 have preceded 5-10% bounces within 2-4 weeks. Invalidation: daily close above 6,750 or VIX below 22.
opportunities.regime_override_justification: Short direction aligns with high-vol downtrend regime. LOW conviction due to Extreme Fear contrarian risk — sharp relief rally possible on any de-escalation headline.
